core_thesis: Husserl's thesis is that philosophy must return to the things themselves by suspending the natural attitude and describing how objectivity is constituted in intentional acts ([Ideas I §§31-32](cite://husserl/ideas-i/31-32); [Cartesian Meditations §13](cite://husserl/cartesian-meditations/13)). Consciousness is always consciousness of something, and the object is given through horizons, profiles, and syntheses rather than as a bare thing outside appearance. Transcendental reduction thus does not abolish the world; it discloses the structures by which the world has sense for consciousness.
